extraction.one_line_summary: PETERSON Mads won sprint from caught breakaway with 200m remaining after CLARK and DE MARCHI waited too long, refusing to commit early.
tactical_patterns: Breakaway held 33 seconds at 3km but lost through hesitation games with 800m left, showing psychological collapse pattern.
THOMAS Garrett mechanical at critical junction forced full team TTT through convoy, revealing GC vulnerability in disorganized finale.
Peloton caught break only at 200m despite 1min+ at 15km, indicating poor chase coordination enables late breaks.

rider_signals.CLARK Simon: negative: 36-year-old hesitated in winning position with 800m remaining, suggesting age/experience paradoxically creates overthinking in breaks
rider_signals.DE MARCHI Alessandro: negative: former Maglia Rosa played waiting games from winning break position instead of committing early
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: dropped on first categorized climb due to knee injury from Stage 5 crash, signaling compromised climbing form
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: late mechanical required team convoy chase in disorganized finale, exposing crash risk when GC protection dissolves
rider_signals.PETERSON Mads: positive: completed Peter Slam launching at 200m from chasing group, showing superior sprint timing over hesitant breakaway
